sessions.lever: removed no-op `do{...}while(0)` + empty `if(0){}` cheat-by-spelling scheduling perturbers (floor 11->6); operand-order/scheduling residual remains

reviewer.evidence: Candidate removes two semantically-empty constructs (do-while(0) wrapper + empty if(0){}) — that REDUCES coercion, a strict cleanup. ZERO cheat-asm. Still carries the 2 regfix reorder rules and is not byte-identical (retire SHA1 mismatch 3fd1338c). Clean WIP, not committed to src.

remaining_gap.summary: Two residual diff clusters after the no-op cleanup (floor 6): (1) the `p_anim = &D_800EF980[arg0]` address compute — target does lui/addiu(base) THEN sll(index) and `addu s0,v1(index),v0(base)`; mine does sll first so index lands in $v0, base in $v1 (operands swapped). (2) one `sh v0,4(s2)` store reordered (the second regfix `reorder 168,169,167 @167-169`).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: The 2 regfix reorder rules (16-insn prologue reorder @3-18 + 3-insn store reorder @167-169). The no-op removal closed 5 of 11 diffs; the remaining 6 are the operand-order + store-scheduling the reorders encode.

next_hypotheses: s0 operand order: get the global BASE computed before the index so base->$v0, index->$v1, `addu s0,v1,v0`. `D_800EF980 + arg0` made it WORSE (11). Try other addressing spellings or compute-order nudges; see compare-operand-order-register.
the `sh v0,4(s2)` store reorder (idx 167-169) is a sched1 store-position diff — try reordering the corresponding source stores in the `var_s3 != 1` block.
decomp-permuter from this floor-6 base (pure operand-order + store-scheduling residual — its specialty). Verify with retire (full SHA1).
